text: 0.1g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a tuber, this mostly lives or dies on carb behaviour.
20.1g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.
1.6g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.
vitamin c at 22% daily value, vitamin a at 18% daily value stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
potassium at 10% daily value stands out most. useful, but still pretty limited.

pros first: practical staple carb source because tubers are often judged by how usable they are day to day. whole-food carb package because that matters because it is a cleaner baseline than heavily refined carbs. can be quite filling because a useful practical strength when the preparation stays sensible.

the drawbacks next: still a carb-heavy category because that is the core ceiling tubers have to manage. preparation changes outcomes a lot because what you do to a tuber can make or break it. protein support is weak because tubers still need help from the rest of the meal.
A tier. Best when you want practical carbs and the rest of the profile is still reasonably clean.
